title: From After-a-Loss Checklist to Full Estate Support
summary: The free After-a-Loss Task Manager on this site covers real ground — the planned Family Executor product is designed to go further, for families who want more support.
subcategory: Privacy, Estate Support, and Getting Started
journeyStage: Privacy, Estate Support, and Getting Started
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: From After-a-Loss Checklist to Full Estate Support | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: The free After-a-Loss Task Manager on this site covers real ground — the planned Family Executor product is designed to go further, for families who want m
whatYouNeedToKnow: The free tool organizes tasks and timing; it doesn't handle document storage or professional coordination.
A more complete product is being developed to bridge into document management and professional referrals.
Nothing about the free tool's usefulness depends on that future product.

paragraphs: The After-a-Loss Task Manager available today genuinely organizes the practical work of the weeks and months after a loss — timing, categories, what to expect — and stands on its own regardless of anything else HarborCare Circle builds.

For families wanting deeper support — secure document storage, professional referrals, more sophisticated multi-person executor coordination — a more complete product is in development, extending rather than replacing what's already free and useful today.

nextSteps: Use the free After-a-Loss Task Manager fully — it's not a limited preview.
If deeper support would help, the waitlist is the way to hear about what's next.
disclaimer: This describes a planned future product, not a currently available feature.
